The Dude (Jeffrey Lebowski).quote: Yeah, well, you know, that's just, like, your opinion, man.
The Dude (Jeffrey Lebowski).scene: The Dude dismisses the Big Lebowski's diatribe in the millionaire's study.

The Dude (Jeffrey Lebowski).quote: The Dude abides.
The Dude (Jeffrey Lebowski).scene: The Dude's parting words to The Stranger at the bowling alley near the film's end.

The Dude (Jeffrey Lebowski).quote: This will not stand, ya know, this aggression will not stand, man.
The Dude (Jeffrey Lebowski).scene: The Dude paraphrases George H.W. Bush while complaining to the Big Lebowski about his ruined rug.

The Dude (Jeffrey Lebowski).quote: Hey, careful, man, there's a beverage here!
The Dude (Jeffrey Lebowski).scene: The Dude protests when one of Treehorn's thugs grabs him in his apartment, spilling his White Russian.

The Dude (Jeffrey Lebowski).quote: That rug really tied the room together.
The Dude (Jeffrey Lebowski).scene: The Dude lamenting his ruined rug to Walter and Donny at the bowling alley.

The Dude (Jeffrey Lebowski).quote: I'm the Dude. So that's what you call me. You know, that or, uh, His Dudeness, or, uh, Duder, or El Duderino if you're not into the whole brevity thing.
The Dude (Jeffrey Lebowski).scene: The Dude introduces himself to the Big Lebowski in his mansion.

The Dude (Jeffrey Lebowski).quote: New shit has come to light.
The Dude (Jeffrey Lebowski).scene: The Dude tries to explain the case to the Big Lebowski after Bunny's apparent kidnapping.

The Dude (Jeffrey Lebowski).quote: You're not wrong, Walter. You're just an asshole.
The Dude (Jeffrey Lebowski).scene: The Dude responds to Walter's righteous tirade outside the bowling alley.

The Dude (Jeffrey Lebowski).quote: Mind if I do a J?
The Dude (Jeffrey Lebowski).scene: The Dude lights up a joint while driving Maude's limo, to the chauffeur's annoyance.

The Dude (Jeffrey Lebowski).quote: Just take it easy, man.
The Dude (Jeffrey Lebowski).scene: The Dude's frequent mantra throughout the film.

The Dude (Jeffrey Lebowski).quote: I can't be worrying about that shit. Life goes on, man.
The Dude (Jeffrey Lebowski).scene: The Dude shrugging off concerns at the bowling alley.

The Dude (Jeffrey Lebowski).quote: This is a very complicated case, Maude. You know, a lotta ins, a lotta outs, a lotta what-have-yous.
The Dude (Jeffrey Lebowski).scene: The Dude tries to update Maude on the kidnapping investigation.

The Dude (Jeffrey Lebowski).quote: Calmer than you are.
The Dude (Jeffrey Lebowski).scene: The Dude responds to Walter's escalating outbursts during a bowling argument.

The Dude (Jeffrey Lebowski).quote: Obviously, you're not a golfer.
The Dude (Jeffrey Lebowski).scene: The Dude observes the thugs trashing his apartment over a bowling ball.

The Dude (Jeffrey Lebowski).quote: Let me explain something to you. Um, I am not 'Mr. Lebowski.' You're Mr. Lebowski. I'm the Dude.
The Dude (Jeffrey Lebowski).scene: The Dude clarifies his identity to the Big Lebowski's assistant Brandt and the millionaire.

The Dude (Jeffrey Lebowski).quote: He fixes the cable?
The Dude (Jeffrey Lebowski).scene: The Dude reacts incredulously when Maude reveals what Knox Harrington does.

The Dude (Jeffrey Lebowski).quote: Sometimes there's a man... I won't say a hero, 'cause, what's a hero?
The Dude (Jeffrey Lebowski).scene: The Stranger narrates the film's opening — though this is The Stranger about The Dude, often quoted as defining him.

The Dude (Jeffrey Lebowski).quote: Walter, I love you, but sooner or later you're going to have to face the fact you're a goddamn moron.
The Dude (Jeffrey Lebowski).scene: The Dude vents at Walter outside the bowling alley after another plan falls apart.

The Dude (Jeffrey Lebowski).quote: At least I'm housebroken.
The Dude (Jeffrey Lebowski).scene: The Dude's deadpan retort after the Big Lebowski calls him a bum.

The Dude (Jeffrey Lebowski).quote: Hey, this is a private residence, man.
The Dude (Jeffrey Lebowski).scene: The Dude protests as Treehorn's thugs barge into his apartment.

The Dude (Jeffrey Lebowski).quote: Mr. Treehorn treats objects like women, man.
The Dude (Jeffrey Lebowski).scene: The Dude muddles his words while high at Jackie Treehorn's beach party.

The Dude (Jeffrey Lebowski).quote: Yeah, well, the Dude abides.
The Dude (Jeffrey Lebowski).scene: The Dude's easygoing shrug, his go-to philosophy throughout the film.

Walter Sobchak.quote: Am I wrong?
Walter Sobchak.scene: Walter's repeated rhetorical question while arguing rules at the bowling alley.

Walter Sobchak.quote: Has the whole world gone crazy? Am I the only one around here who gives a shit about the rules?
Walter Sobchak.scene: Walter loses it after Smokey crosses the foul line during league play.

Walter Sobchak.quote: MARK IT ZERO!
Walter Sobchak.scene: Walter screams at Smokey to invalidate his score, gun drawn, in the bowling alley.

Walter Sobchak.quote: Smokey, my friend, you are entering a world of pain.
Walter Sobchak.scene: Walter warns Smokey at the bowling alley over a contested foul line.

Walter Sobchak.quote: This is not 'Nam. This is bowling. There are rules.
Walter Sobchak.scene: Walter explains the seriousness of bowling to Smokey.

Walter Sobchak.quote: You're entering a world of pain.
Walter Sobchak.scene: Walter's frequent threat throughout the film.

Walter Sobchak.quote: Forget it, Donny, you're out of your element!
Walter Sobchak.scene: Walter dismisses Donny's contributions during arguments at the bowling alley.

Walter Sobchak.quote: Shut the fuck up, Donny!
Walter Sobchak.scene: Walter's recurring response whenever Donny tries to chime in.

Walter Sobchak.quote: Saturday, Donny, is Shabbos, the Jewish day of rest. That means I don't work, I don't drive a car, I don't fucking ride in a car, I don't handle money, I don't turn on the oven, and I sure as shit don't fucking roll!
Walter Sobchak.scene: Walter explains why he can't bowl on Saturday, despite having converted for his ex-wife.

Walter Sobchak.quote: Fuck it, Dude. Let's go bowling.
Walter Sobchak.scene: Walter's resolution after the case spirals into chaos near the end of the film.

Walter Sobchak.quote: I'm staying. Finishing my coffee. Enjoying my coffee.
Walter Sobchak.scene: Walter refuses to leave the diner after being asked to lower his voice.

Walter Sobchak.quote: Say what you want about the tenets of National Socialism, Dude, at least it's an ethos.
Walter Sobchak.scene: Walter contrasts the nihilists with Nazis after they confront the Dude.

Walter Sobchak.quote: Nihilists! Fuck me. I mean, say what you like about the tenets of National Socialism, Dude, at least it's an ethos.
Walter Sobchak.scene: Walter rants about the German nihilists threatening the Dude.

Walter Sobchak.quote: Eight-year-olds, Dude.
Walter Sobchak.scene: Walter explains his suspicion of the schoolboy Larry Sellers.

Walter Sobchak.quote: Life does not stop and start at your convenience, you miserable piece of shit.
Walter Sobchak.scene: Walter berates Larry Sellers in his bedroom while attempting to recover the money.

Walter Sobchak.quote: Do you see what happens, Larry? Do you see what happens when you fuck a stranger in the ass?
Walter Sobchak.scene: Walter destroys what he believes is Larry's new car with a crowbar.

Walter Sobchak.quote: He's a good man. And thorough.
Walter Sobchak.scene: Walter describing the private detective Da Fino sarcastically.

Walter Sobchak.quote: You want a toe? I can get you a toe, believe me. There are ways, Dude. You don't wanna know about it, believe me.
Walter Sobchak.scene: Walter dismisses the severed toe as proof of anything during the kidnapping case.

Walter Sobchak.quote: Donny was a good bowler, and a good man.
Walter Sobchak.scene: Walter's rambling eulogy for Donny on the cliffside.

Walter Sobchak.quote: Over the line!
Walter Sobchak.scene: Walter calls Smokey's foot fault during league play, refusing to let it slide.

Walter Sobchak.quote: Were you listening to the Dude's story, Donny?
Walter Sobchak.scene: Walter cuts Donny off for not following the conversation at the bowling alley.

Donny Kerabatsos.scene: Donny chimes in during a bowling alley conversation, having misheard the topic entirely.
Donny notices the phone ringing in the bowling alley.
Donny innocently asks for clarification after Walter insults Jesus Quintana.
Donny asks Walter about the menacing Germans following the Dude.
Donny excited about bowling well during league play.

The Stranger.quote: Sometimes you eat the bear, and sometimes, well, he eats you.
The Stranger.scene: The Stranger offers folksy wisdom to the Dude at the bowling alley bar.

The Stranger.quote: Take 'er easy, Dude. I know that you will.
The Stranger.scene: The Stranger's farewell to the Dude near the end of the film.

The Stranger.quote: The Dude abides. I don't know about you, but I take comfort in that. It's good knowin' he's out there.
The Stranger.scene: The Stranger's closing narration to the camera.

The Stranger.quote: Sir, I have to ask you to watch the language.
The Stranger.scene: The Stranger gently chides the Dude for cursing at the bowling alley bar.

The Stranger.quote: Do you have to use so many cuss words?
The Stranger.scene: The Stranger gently chides the Dude for cursing at the bowling alley bar.

The Stranger.quote: Sometimes there's a man, well, he's the man for his time and place. He fits right in there.
The Stranger.scene: The Stranger's opening narration introducing the Dude.

The Stranger.quote: Wal, that about does her, wraps her all up.
The Stranger.scene: The Stranger's closing narration as the film winds down.

The Stranger.quote: Darkness warshed over the Dude — darker'n a black steer's tookus on a moonless prairie night. There was no bottom.
The Stranger.scene: The Stranger narrates as the Dude is knocked unconscious.

The Stranger.quote: I lost my train of thought here.
The Stranger.scene: The Stranger trails off mid-narration at the bowling alley bar.

The Stranger.quote: Catch ya later on down the trail.
The Stranger.scene: The Stranger's folksy farewell at the end of the film.

Jesus Quintana.scene: Jesus delivers his iconic warning to the Dude and Walter at the bowling alley.

Jesus Quintana.quote: You said it, man. Nobody fucks with the Jesus.
Jesus Quintana.scene: Jesus reiterates his threat after polishing his bowling ball.

Jesus Quintana.quote: You ready to be fucked, man?
Jesus Quintana.scene: Jesus taunts the Dude's team about their upcoming match.

Jesus Quintana.quote: I see you rolled your way into the semis. Dios mio, man. Liam and me, we're gonna fuck you up.
Jesus Quintana.scene: Jesus confronts the Dude and Walter at the bowling alley.

Maude Lebowski.quote: The word itself makes some men uncomfortable. Vagina.
Maude Lebowski.scene: Maude lectures the Dude about feminist art in her loft studio.

Maude Lebowski.quote: Does the female form make you uncomfortable, Mr. Lebowski?
Maude Lebowski.scene: Maude challenges the Dude while showing him her artwork.
Maude clinically describes what she expects of the Dude during their conversation.

Maude Lebowski.quote: Lord. You can imagine where it goes from here.
Maude Lebowski.scene: Maude on her father's history with women, while showing the Dude an old photo.

The Big Lebowski (Jeffrey Lebowski).quote: Your revolution is over, Mr. Lebowski. Condolences. The bums lost!
The Big Lebowski (Jeffrey Lebowski).scene: The Big Lebowski rails at the Dude in his mansion study.

The Big Lebowski (Jeffrey Lebowski).quote: The bums will always lose, do you hear me, Lebowski?
The Big Lebowski (Jeffrey Lebowski).scene: The millionaire's tirade about the Dude's lifestyle.

The Big Lebowski (Jeffrey Lebowski).quote: What makes a man, Mr. Lebowski?
The Big Lebowski (Jeffrey Lebowski).scene: The Big Lebowski's pompous question to the Dude during their first meeting.

The Big Lebowski (Jeffrey Lebowski).quote: Strong men also cry. Strong men also cry.
The Big Lebowski (Jeffrey Lebowski).scene: The Big Lebowski tearfully tells the Dude about Bunny's supposed kidnapping.

Brandt.quote: Her life is in your hands, Dude.
Brandt.scene: Brandt anxiously hands the Dude the ransom briefcase.

Brandt.quote: Lots of, uh, lots of achievements, Dude.
Brandt.scene: Brandt nervously tours the Dude through the Big Lebowski's wall of accomplishments.
Brandt's rehearsed enthusiasm during the Dude's tour of the mansion.

Nihilists (Uli, Kieffer, Franz).scene: The nihilists confront the Dude in his apartment with a ferret.

Nihilists (Uli, Kieffer, Franz).quote: We believe in nothing, Lebowski! Nothing!
Nihilists (Uli, Kieffer, Franz).scene: The nihilists shout at the Dude during the parking lot showdown.
Uli threatens the Dude in his bathtub while dropping a ferret in the water.

Bunny Lebowski.quote: I'll suck your cock for a thousand dollars.
Bunny Lebowski.scene: Bunny propositions the Dude on her first appearance at the Lebowski mansion pool.

Jackie Treehorn.quote: People forget that the brain is the biggest erogenous zone.
Jackie Treehorn.scene: Treehorn entertains the Dude at his Malibu beach party.
Treehorn vaguely describes his line of work to the Dude.

Da Fino.scene: Da Fino introduces himself to the Dude as a fellow private detective in the Dude's car.
